steps.title: Reverse the LNH prediction
steps.content: The linguistic niche hypothesis predicts an inverse relationship: as exotericity increases, grammatical complexity should decrease. Fusion and informativity are positive indices of grammatical complexity, so evidence inconsistent with the hypothesis would connect those two features positively with exotericity.

steps.title: Focus on exotericity
steps.content: Choices A and C compare grammatical-complexity measures with each other, but they do not address exotericity, the key variable in the LNH. Choice B gives a negative relationship between grammatical complexity and exotericity, which is exactly what the LNH predicts. A positive correlation between exotericity and both fusion and informativity would mean complexity indicators rise as non-native speaker prevalence rises, contradicting the predicted inverse relationship. The answer is D.
